title: China’s Big Bet on Wind Power Is Paying Off
summary: An industrial policy of subsidies and import restrictions laid the foundations for China to become almost as dominant in wind turbines as in solar panels.

title: What We Know About ‘Project Freedom’ in the Strait of Hormuz
summary: Violence flared in the waterway on Monday after President Trump said the United States would help guide stranded ships. It’s not clear exactly how the plan is working.

title: The Growing Rift Between the UAE and Saudi Arabia, Explained
summary: Officials say competition between the countries is healthy. But tensions are mounting over energy quotas, regional conflicts and their different visions for the Middle East.

title: Trump Administration Investigating Smith College Over Transgender Admissions
summary: The Education Department’s civil rights arm said admitting “biological males” to the women’s college may violate anti-discrimination laws.

title: U.S. Trade Deficit Grew in March
summary: Both exports and imports rose after the Supreme Court struck down many of the president’s highest levies in February.

title: These 7 Primary Elections Will Test President Trump’s Power in Indiana
summary: The president is seeking political payback for Republican state legislators in Indiana who rejected his push to redraw the state’s political map last year.

title: President Trump Seeks Retribution in Republican Primaries
summary: President Trump is endorsing candidates in a series of primary elections this month, trying to defeat incumbents in his own party who have crossed him. Our national political correspondent Shane Goldmacher explains.

title: Paxton’s Fund-Raising Struggles in Texas Underscore Deep Rift in G.O.P.
summary: The Texas attorney general is heading into the runoff against Senator John Cornyn without the support of some of his longtime backers.

title: A Top 2026 Senate Race Kicks Off With Attacks About Jeffrey Epstein
summary: Ohio’s unusual contest — with a little-known incumbent and a well-known challenger — shows how Democrats are hoping to capitalize on G.O.P. voters’ anger at the Jeffrey Epstein scandal.

title: Was It Art? Was It Fashion? Was It Good?
summary: Guests at the Met Gala had different interpretations of the night’s dress code.

title: When ‘The Late Show With Stephen Colbert’ Goes Away, What Do We Lose?
summary: Our attachment to an institution may seem counterintuitive, especially with comedy, a rebellious art form. But with Stephen Colbert’s program, there was a lot at stake.

title: The Federal Safety Net Isn’t Ready for Artificial Intelligence
summary: As fears of A.I.-driven job losses mount, economists warn that unemployment benefits and other programs to help displaced workers aren’t sufficient.

title: U.S.C. Will Infuse A.I. Across University with $200 Million Donation
summary: The University of Southern California wants to integrate artificial intelligence into fields like health care and the arts. Other universities are also using donations to focus more on A.I.

title: Coinbase Lays Off 14% of Employees as A.I. Changes Work
summary: The largest U.S. crypto exchange said it was cutting jobs because of cryptocurrency market volatility and to “optimize” for the artificial intelligence era.

title: Five Publishers and Scott Turow Sue Meta and Mark Zuckerberg
summary: The class-action lawsuit accuses the tech giant and its founder and chief executive of infringing on authors’ copyrights.

title: Inside Meatstock, the Carnivore Diet Convention Where Steak Is Medicine
summary: Devotees of the diet, which Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy, Jr. follows, bonded over brisket and butter at Meatstock.

title: Modi’s Triumph in West Bengal Elections Puts Him Closer to an Opposition-Free India
summary: With his triumph in West Bengal state elections, Prime Minister Narendra Modi has moved closer to his dream of an opposition-free India.

title: James Murdoch’s Company Said to Be in Talks to Acquire Major Parts of Vox Media
summary: A deal for Vox Media, which generates more than $80 million through podcasts alone, would elevate Mr. Murdoch’s company, Lupa Systems, as a major player in U.S. media.

title: A Portfolio of Work From The Times’s Pulitzer Prize-Winning Photographer
summary: The photographer Saher Alghorra reported on the Gaza conflict while enduring the dire conditions his images portrayed.

title: The FBI Searched a Washington Post Reporter’s Home. She Is Now a Pulitzer Prize Winner.
summary: Hannah Natanson’s reporting anchored a package of articles from The Washington Post that won a Pulitzer on Monday, four months after agents seized her devices.

title: In Mamdani’s Rise, Muslim Organizers See Political Opportunity
summary: The Muslim Democratic Club was founded to increase political power among the city’s Muslims. Now some of its founders are the mayor’s top aides.

title: Vornado C.E.O. Blasts Mamdani’s Tax-the-Rich Approach in Earnings Call
summary: Steven Roth, the chief executive of Vornado, criticized Mayor Zohran Mamdani for singling out the billionaire Ken Griffin in his bid to make the wealthy pay more taxes in New York City.

title: New Musicals ‘Schmigadoon!’ and ‘Lost Boys’ Lead List of Tony Nominees
summary: The nominated performers include the film stars Daniel Radcliffe, Rose Byrne, John Lithgow and Lesley Manville.

title: Tony Awards Nominations 2026: The Complete List
summary: Nominations for the 79th Tony Awards were announced on Tuesday. Here’s who made the list.

title: Tony Nominations Snubs and Surprises: Lea Michele Left Out, ‘Titaníque’ Shines
summary: Lea Michele, Adrien Brody and other boldface names were left out, while June Squibb, André De Shields and Layton Williams as an iceberg were among the surprises.

title: Cruise Ship Struck by Hantavirus Is to Head to Canary Islands, W.H.O. Says
summary: Spain said it would decide on a port of call only after disease experts inspected the vessel. The World Health Organization said human-to-human transmission may have played a role in the outbreak.

title: What to Know About the Hantavirus Outbreak on an Atlantic Cruise Ship
summary: Health authorities said three passengers from the MV Hondius died after showing symptoms of the rare disease, with another critically ill and three more showing mild symptoms.

title: This Trump Proposal is Good for Private Equity — and Not Good for Our Retirement Accounts
summary: A win for private equity. A loss for our retirement assets.

title: The Book That Changed How I Think About Liberalism
summary: The historian Helena Rosenblatt traces the roots of liberalism and the values that many have forgotten.

title: The Best Looks From the Met Gala
summary: We’re covering the cobblestone carpet.

title: U.S. Sues The New York Times, Claiming Discrimination Against a White Man
summary: The Equal Employment Opportunity Commission said the paper had engaged in “unlawful employment practices” against the man, who did not get a sought-after promotion.

title: Delta Air Lines Will Stop Serving Snacks and Drinks on Short Flights
summary: Most travelers flying less than 350 miles on Delta will soon have to go without free coffee and cookies, but the carrier is adding service to longer flights.

title: Secret Service Agent John Spillman Charged With Indecent Exposure at Miami Hotel
summary: The officer, who was in Miami for an event President Trump was attending over the weekend, was seen masturbating in the hallway, the police said.

title: Fireworks Factory Explosion in China Kills at Least 26
summary: Dozens more were injured in the explosion, which appeared to be one of the country’s deadliest in recent years.
